question.text: SEBI regulates the securities market under the SEBI Act, 1992. After an amendment, SEBI's jurisdiction was expanded to also regulate:

answer.text: Commodity derivatives, included in the definition of securities under SCRA 1956

explanation.text: **The Logic:** After the merger of the Forward Markets Commission (FMC) with SEBI in 2015, commodity derivatives were brought under SEBI's regulatory purview. This was facilitated by amending the Securities Contracts (Regulation) Act, 1956 (SCRA) to include commodity derivatives in the definition of "securities." **The Trap:** "Banking and money market" remains under RBI. "Insurance and pension" are regulated by IRDAI and PFRDA respectively. "Foreign exchange transactions" fall under RBI through FEMA. Each of these has a distinct regulator. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX tests knowledge of all financial regulators and their jurisdictions. The FMC-SEBI merger is also tested in NISM Series XIII (Common Derivatives) certification. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The SEBI-FMC merger unified the derivatives regulatory framework in India — now equity, currency, interest rate, AND commodity derivatives are all regulated by one entity (SEBI), creating a simpler compliance environment for multi-product intermediaries.
